title: Solanecio mannii
intro: Solanecio mannii, also known as East African needle bush, is a shrubby plant species native to East Africa, characterized by its needle-like leaves and small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Solanecio mannii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Solanecio mannii include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat fever, skin conditions, and other health issues.
taxonomy: Solanecio mannii is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the tribe Plucheeae, a group of flowering plants that are often found in Africa. The genus Solanecio is part of the subfamily Carduoideae, which includes thistles and related species. Solanecio mannii is native to the tropical regions of Africa, where it grows in a variety of habitats. This classification indicates that Solanecio mannii is a member of a diverse and widespread group of flowering plants.
